channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Native duck species at risk of disappearing, conservationists warn
item.description: The crossbreeding of Pacific black ducks with introduced species has devastated the native species in New Zealand — and conservationists say there's a risk that's happening in Tasmania too.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Heard

media:group.media:description: For those wanting to keep ducks on their properties, it is recommended Muscovy ducks and guinea fowls, which don't pose the same hybridisation risk as mallards.

item.title: Record fuel costs do little to deter big car buyers
item.description: Electric vehicles now represent 16 per cent of new car sales in Australia, in a growing trend coinciding with record fuel prices.
item.dc:creator: Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: Rising fuel costs have had little impact on sales of big cars.

item.title: Another Aussie out of Giro as UAE Team Emirates hits back
item.description: Jhonatan Narváez dedicated his Giro d'Italia stage four victory to his fallen UAE Team Emirates colleagues as another Aussie fell by the wayside as the race hit Italian soil.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Kaden Groves was banged up and far from his best after the first-stage crash.

item.title: Australia insists support for UAE does not involve action against Iran
item.description: The Albanese government insists Australia is not involved in any "offensive action against Iran" amid reports the UAE, which had been supplied a Wedgetail aircraft, carried out military strikes against the country.
item.dc:creator: Laura Tingle

media:group.media:description: Australia's E-7A Wedgetail aircraft have a radar with a range of 400 kilometres.

item.title: How Australians across generations feel about the budget
item.description: The ABC has asked Australians across generations, from gen Z to baby boomers, about the latest federal budget.
item.dc:creator: Ahmed Yussuf

media:group.media:description: The ABC spoke to people across generations to find out how they feel about the federal budget.

item.title: Will property tax changes level the playing field or raise the drawbridge?
item.description: There's no doubt that taking away negative gearing on existing properties will reduce the amount many investors can afford to pay, evening up the playing field for owner-occupiers. But these tax changes by themselves aren't a cure all.
item.dc:creator: Michael Janda

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers have decided to take a "seek forgiveness, not permission" approach to housing changes.

item.title: Quiz: Dig out your passport, it's time for a whirlwind world trip
item.description: Where was the world's first permanent commercial bungee jumping site set up? And which country is home to the historic city of Timbuktu?

media:group.media:description: A man in mid-air during a bungee jump.

item.title: After his recital was canned, Jayson Gillham took an orchestra to court
item.description: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Story

media:group.media:description: On the cusp of returning to Australia as the plaintiff in a 15-day trial, Jayson Gillham has announced a new national tour.

item.title: How Broadbeach became a blues mecca
item.description: As some festivals crumble, the Gold Coast's mayor says the 25-year-old Blues On Broadbeach event isn't going anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Dan Condon

media:group.media:description: Blues On Broadbeach celebrates its 25th anniversary this weekend.

item.title: Labor bets the house on young Australians' desperation to buy their own
item.description: If the reforms help younger Australians believe home ownership is within reach, Labor can expect to collect a political dividend.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers have made generational equity the test for this budget.

item.title: Starmer faces more calls to quit as ministers resign
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer is striking a defiant tone despite a growing number of his Labour colleagues urging him to step down.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Morgan

media:group.media:description: Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer is refusing to resign despite mounting pressure from Labour ranks.

item.title: $250 tax break for millions in budget's 'rebalance' of system for workers
item.description: Labor will raise taxes on investment properties and some trust funds, giving the revenue back to workers with a new $250 tax break unveiled in the federal budget.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers and Finance Minister Katy Gallagher say the budget aims to tackle intergenerational inequity.

item.title: Why Chalmers reined in the property tax lurks in the budget
item.description: One of the more striking elements from the now well-entrenched program of pre-budget leaks is the lack of outrage from the population or even the opposition.
item.dc:creator: Ian Verrender

media:group.media:description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers said in his budget speech that the government had chosen "the hard road of reform, not the path of least resistance".

item.title: How CGT changes could push investors to look outside property
item.description: Investors in stocks will be affected by changes to capital gains tax, but experts say the budget is still likely to see some investment move away from property and into shares.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Chalmers, Jasper Wells, and David Taylor

media:group.media:description: Several tax measures to help small business with cash flow have been made permanent in the budget.

item.title: Australia's last baby boomer prime minister becomes King of the Kids
item.description: Tonight, Anthony Albanese becomes the first serving prime minister in decades to take a hammer to the most sacred shibboleth in Australian domestic politics.
item.dc:creator: Annabel Crabb

media:group.media:description: Millennial and generation Z voters now constitute a majority of the electorate.

item.title: Your five-minute guide to this year's cost-of-living budget measures
item.description: Fresh tax cuts, an instant $1,000 tax reduction and cheaper medicines: here's what the federal government's latest budget means for Australians.
item.dc:creator: Hanan Dervisevic

media:group.media:description: The federal budget will target cost of living in what Treasurer Jim Chalmers says will "take pressure off Australians".

item.title: Negative gearing cut off for existing homes bought after budget night
item.description: Tax perks that property investors have used for decades to maximise profits and offset their losses will be restricted from tonight, in one of the most significant changes to the tax system in years.
item.dc:creator: Jake Evans

media:group.media:description: Decades-old tax perks for property investors are being restricted, and loopholes allowing wealthy families to pay less tax are being closed.

item.title: Find out if you're among the budget's winners or losers
item.description: In the first full budget since Labor's landslide election victory last year, the government is putting some money back in workers' pockets and increasing taxes on investors. So who wins and who misses out?
item.dc:creator: Holly Tregenza and Samantha Dick

item.title: Father allegedly high on 'nangs' in crash that killed daughters, as scuffle erupts outside court
item.description: A Perth father was allegedly high on 'nangs' during a crash that killed his two daughters, as a scuffle with media erupts at court.
item.dc:creator: Mya Kordic

media:group.media:description: The man is facing Midland Magistrates Court afte being charged with causing the girls' death.

item.title: The name of this health condition was 'very inaccurate'. Now it's changed
item.description: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) has been renamed polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS), a move health experts say will lead to faster diagnoses and better treatment for women of all ages.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Roberts and Anna Chisholm

media:group.media:description: About one in eight women have the complex lifelong condition.

item.title: LeBron contemplates potential retirement after NBA playoff exit
item.description: LeBron James says he has no idea what the future holds after being bounced out of the second round of the NBA playoffs.

media:group.media:description: LeBron James is still undecided on whether he will continue his NBA career into a 24th season after being eliminated from the play-offs.

item.title: Duo swindled $6 million from five victims in investment scam, court hears
item.description: Two men fleeced millions from five victims in a fraudulent property investment scheme, which one victim said had stolen half her superannuation and "ruined" her life.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: Andre Langos and Sam Smith will be sentenced in South Australia's District Court next month.

item.title: Rankine says he has 'come a long way' since homophobic slur ban
item.description: After being forced to sit out of last year's finals series thanks to a suspension for a homophobic slur, Izak Rankine is confident he has grown.

media:group.media:description: Izak Rankine is relishing being back in the spotlight.

item.title: Property owner says he helped wounded officer after Dezi Freeman shot at police
item.description: A Porepunkah property owner says he helped a wounded police officer after the fatal shooting when Dezi Freeman killed two Victoria police officers.
item.dc:creator: Ashlee Aldridge

media:group.media:description: The owner of the property where two police officers were shot dead speaks out in new video.

item.title: Pakistan market blast kills at least nine people
item.description: A blast in a market kills at least nine people as Islamabad blames Kabul for a weekend attack that has left 15 police officers dead.

media:group.media:description: Police officers and local residents inspect the damage at the site of a bomb explosion.
